answer:
The void ratio is \( e = \frac{V_{\text{voids}}}{V_{\text{grains}}} = 0.80 \), so the total volume is \( V_{\text{total}} = V_{\text{grains}} + V_{\text{voids}} = V_{\text{grains}}(1+e) \).  
The mass is from the grains only: \( m = \rho_{\text{SiO}_2} V_{\text{grains}} \).  
Thus, the sand density is  
\[
\rho_{\text{sand}} = \frac{m}{V_{\text{total}}} = \frac{\rho_{\text{SiO}_2}}{1+e} = \frac{2600}{1.80} \approx 1444 \, \text{kg/m}^3,
\]  
which rounds to \( 1.4 \times 10^3 \, \text{kg/m}^3 \).  

\boxed{A}
